original: Patient is a 55-year-old Caucasian male presenting with acute chest pain. BMI is 29. No significant family history of early heart disease.
shifted: Patient is a 48-year-old Indian male presenting with acute chest pain. BMI is 26, but he exhibits significant central adiposity (waist circumference 102 cm) and has a known history of prediabetes, indicative of metabolic syndrome. He reports a strong family history of premature coronary artery disease, with his father having an MI at age 52 and a maternal uncle undergoing CABG at 55. His mother also has type 2 diabetes and hypertension. A prior lipid panel revealed elevated Lipoprotein(a) [Lp(a)] levels. Management will align with Indian National Consensus guidelines on CVD.

original: 60-year-old female with suspected hypertrophic cardiomyopathy. Echo shows septal hypertrophy. BMI 26.
shifted: 55-year-old female with suspected hypertrophic cardiomyopathy. Echocardiogram shows significant septal hypertrophy. Her BMI is 22 kg/m², but she presents with notable central adiposity and features consistent with metabolic syndrome. A strong family history of early-onset heart failure and sudden cardiac death in first-degree relatives raises suspicion for a genetic predisposition, specifically warranting consideration for the MYBPC3 Δ25bp variant, given its higher prevalence in the South Asian population. Clinical management will emphasize early intervention and aggressive risk factor modification, aligning with Indian National Consensus guidelines for CVD.
